<!-- Generated by dart run tool/visibility/generate_visibility.dart. Edit tool/visibility/visibility_manifest.json instead. -->
# pqforge: Post-Quantum Application Recipes

Canonical URL: https://turkananation.github.io/pqforge/
Repository: https://github.com/turkananation/pqforge
Package: https://pub.dev/packages/pqforge
API docs: https://pub.dev/documentation/pqforge/latest/
Version: 0.1.0

## Identity

pqforge turns pqcrypto ML-KEM and ML-DSA primitives into application-ready envelopes, signatures, hybrid key agreement, wrapped key custody, and a local/server CLI.

## Capabilities

- ML-KEM-512/768/1024 KEM-DEM envelopes through pqcrypto
- ML-DSA-44/65/87 detached signatures through pqcrypto
- AES-256-GCM and ChaCha20-Poly1305 secure sessions
- X25519 plus ML-KEM built-in hybrid key agreement
- ML-DSA plus Ed25519 built-in hybrid signatures
- Argon2id plus AES-GCM wrapped key custody
- Named recipes for files, folders, text, media, email, documents, webhooks, tokens, records, logs, artifacts, and identity bindings
- Universal CLI for local and server encryption, decryption, signing, and verification

## Does Not Provide

- CMVP/FIPS 140 module validation
- A hard constant-time guarantee for Dart runtimes
- A hard memory-erasure guarantee
- Public-key trust, identity vetting, or legal e-signature policy
- Replay stores, authorization policy, TLS, or secure infrastructure operations
- RC4 or unauthenticated encryption

## Evidence Boundary

The package provides application composition around pqcrypto evidence. It is not a validated cryptographic module.

Allowed claims:
- FIPS 203-aligned ML-KEM through pqcrypto.
- FIPS 204-aligned ML-DSA through pqcrypto.
- Application-layer composition helpers for KEM-DEM, AEAD sessions, wrapped key custody, signatures, recipes, and CLI workflows.
- Best-effort zeroization in Dart.

Forbidden claims:
- FIPS validated
- FIPS 140 validated
- CMVP validated
- certified
- hard constant-time Dart guarantee
- hard memory-erasure guarantee
- ML-KEM alone is secure transport
- AES signs documents
- RC4 is supported

## Profiles

- compact: ML-KEM-512 + ML-DSA-44 for smaller demos, constrained payloads, and low-bandwidth signatures.
- balanced: ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 for default application and server profile.
- maximum: ML-KEM-1024 + ML-DSA-87 for long-lived records, archives, media, and high-value artifacts.

## Recipe Catalog

### Local File Vault

- Surface: CLI: keygen, encrypt, decrypt
- Apps: developer machines, backup jobs, admin laptops, server batch jobs
- APIs: encryptFileBytes, decryptFileBytes, PqEnvelope
- Caveat: Use wrapped secret keys and external storage policy.

### Folder Archive

- Surface: CLI: encrypt-folder, decrypt-folder
- Apps: case files, release bundles, evidence folders, internal archives
- APIs: encryptFolderEntry, decryptFolderEntry
- Caveat: Each file is an independent envelope; design streaming for very large files.

### Text Snippets

- Surface: CLI: encrypt-text, decrypt-text, sign --kind text
- Apps: notes, secrets, prompts, policy snippets, short messages
- APIs: sealText, openText, signText, verifyText
- Caveat: UTF-8 text only; binary data should use file or media recipes.

### Media Assets

- Surface: CLI: encrypt-media, decrypt-media, sign --kind media
- Apps: photos, audio, video, PDFs, campaign media, identity images
- APIs: sealMedia, openMedia, signMedia, verifyMedia
- Caveat: Bind MIME type and media id; classify content before storing.

### Document Signing

- Surface: CLI: sign, verify
- Apps: contracts, reports, certificates, approvals, policy documents
- APIs: signDocument, verifyDocument
- Caveat: Application owns legal policy and signer identity vetting.

### Webhook Integrity

- Surface: library
- Apps: payment callbacks, audit delivery, server-to-server events
- APIs: signWebhook, verifyWebhook
- Caveat: Application owns replay windows and key publication.

### Signed Tokens

- Surface: library
- Apps: API capabilities, admin actions, short-lived grants
- APIs: issueToken, verifyToken
- Caveat: Application owns revocation and authorization policy.

### Private Email Payloads

- Surface: library
- Apps: encrypted mail bodies, secure notifications, outbound archive mail
- APIs: sealEmail, openEmail
- Caveat: Transport headers and metadata may remain visible unless the app hides them.

### Government And Medical Records

- Surface: library
- Apps: public-sector records, patient records, registry payloads
- APIs: encryptRecord, appendSignedLogEntry
- Caveat: Application owns access control, retention, and audit policy.

### Software Artifacts

- Surface: CLI/library
- Apps: release bundles, firmware, packages, configuration rollouts
- APIs: signArtifact, verifyArtifact
- Caveat: Sign version and target metadata to block rollback replay.

### Hybrid Server Sessions

- Surface: cryptography entrypoint
- Apps: Serverpod, API services, local agents, backend handshakes
- APIs: PqForgeHybridKeyAgreement, PqForgeSecureSession
- Caveat: Authenticate public key bundles and design replay/session storage.

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/turkananation/pqforge/blob/feat/hybrid-combiner-secure-session/README.md): High-signal overview, badges, quickstarts, recipe map, and validation commands.
- [CLI Guide](https://github.com/turkananation/pqforge/blob/feat/hybrid-combiner-secure-session/doc/CLI.md): Full command-line usage for wrapped keys, file, folder, text, media, and signatures.
- [Project Catalog](https://github.com/turkananation/pqforge/blob/feat/hybrid-combiner-secure-session/doc/cookbook/PROJECT_CATALOG.md): Applications and domains that map to pqforge recipes.
- [Recipe Cookbook](https://github.com/turkananation/pqforge/blob/feat/hybrid-combiner-secure-session/doc/cookbook/README.md): Named recipe docs for safe application composition.
- [API Reference](https://github.com/turkananation/pqforge/blob/feat/hybrid-combiner-secure-session/doc/API.md): Facade methods, hybrid tier, secure sessions, types, and CLI summary.
- [Hybrid Audit](https://github.com/turkananation/pqforge/blob/feat/hybrid-combiner-secure-session/doc/HYBRID_AUDIT.md): Coverage audit against pqcrypto building blocks and explicit rejections.
- [Key Custody](https://github.com/turkananation/pqforge/blob/feat/hybrid-combiner-secure-session/doc/architecture/KEY_CUSTODY.md): Wrapped key storage and pluggable custody model.
- [Claim Boundary](https://github.com/turkananation/pqforge/blob/feat/hybrid-combiner-secure-session/doc/security/CLAIM_BOUNDARY.md): Allowed and forbidden claims for package consumers and agents.

## Validation

- `dart run tool/visibility/generate_visibility.dart --check`
- `dart format --output=none --set-exit-if-changed .`
- `dart analyze`
- `dart test`
- `dart run example/pqforge_example.dart`
- `dart run example/catalog_recipes_example.dart`
- `dart run example/hybrid_key_agreement_example.dart`
- `dart pub publish --dry-run`
